text:
When you write code, bugs are inevitable. Identifying bugs means finding where your program behaves unexpectedly. A systematic approach helps you solve problems efficiently: first, reproduce the error consistently, then narrow down the possible causes.

Using debugging tools makes this process easier. Modern development environments often have built-in debuggers that let you pause code execution, inspect variables, and step through lines one by one. Another useful strategy is logging—adding simple print statements or log messages to track what your program does at key points. Logging is especially helpful for understanding issues that happen occasionally or in production environments. By combining a step-by-step method with these tools, you can fix bugs more quickly and learn from each mistake.
